response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has 20+ years of broad software engineering experience, but his expertise is overwhelmingly in web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than C++. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments and a university thesis project — far short of the sustained, deep C++/STL, multi-threading, and systems-level proficiency this role demands as its primary technical core. He also lacks all domain-critical requirements: no IEC 62443 industrial-cybersecurity knowledge, no MQTT or industrial-protocol experience (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0), and no industrial-automation or IoT background.
strengths: Long career demonstrating strong software craftsmanship, TDD discipline, agile practices, and a track record of mentoring
Event-driven architecture and distributed-systems exposure, including high-throughput microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic
Breadth with REST APIs, Node.js, and cloud tooling (AWS CDK), partially aligning with the role's advantageous skills
concerns: C++ proficiency is minimal — approximately six months at National Instruments plus a thesis project; the role requires C++/STL as its primary, deep technical competency
No industrial-cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443, SDL) and no exposure to industrial protocols such as Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, MQTT, or IEC 61850
Education is a BS in Computer Science and a Fine Arts degree, not an engineering degree (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech) as specified by the role

key_factors.factor: Strong software craftsmanship and agile practices
